# backend/tests/test_rbac_middleware.py
# auto-generated explainer · 2026-06-05T10:00:47Z

## Purpose
test_rbac_middleware — permission matrix enforcement for Sales + AI endpoints.

Covers:
- All four roles allowed on GET /stores
- /simulate restricted to manager (other 3 roles → 403)
- Unknown X-Demo-Role → 400
- Missing X-Demo-Role → defaults to manager (200)
- Paths not in matrix pass through unchanged

## Imports
- from `__future__` import annotations
- from `_` import pytest
- from `fastapi.testclient` import TestClient
- from `main` import app

## Inputs
(no CLI / env inputs — library module or implicit)

## Process
### Functions
- `client()`: (no docstring)
- `test_stores_allowed_for_all_roles(client)`: (no docstring)
- `test_simulate_manager_only(client)`: (no docstring)
- `test_unknown_role_returns_400(client)`: (no docstring)
- `test_missing_role_defaults_to_manager(client)`: (no docstring)
- `test_non_matrix_path_is_allowed(client)`: (no docstring)
- `test_supply_chain_simulate_manager_only(client)`: Network simulation is manager-only — matches the Sales /simulate pattern.
- `test_supply_chain_stockout_allowed_for_all_roles(client)`: Read-ish endpoints are open to all four demo roles.
- `test_tester_can_hit_stores(client)`: Tester (read-only role) is permitted on GET /sales/stores — expect non-403/400.
- `test_tester_cannot_simulate(client)`: Simulation remains manager-only — tester should be 403 with FORBIDDEN.

## Outputs
- Return values from functions above

## Notable inline comments
- /stores may return 404/503 if fact_sales empty, but MUST NOT be 403
- Manager: should not be 403 (actual 2xx/4xx depends on data — just not RBAC-blocked)
- All three non-manager roles → 403
- No X-Demo-Role header → middleware defaults to manager → not 403/400
- A path NOT in PERMS_MATRIX should pass through regardless of role.
